content.text: If I was a marketing leader or a founder
needing more demos in the next quarter,
I'd ignore all the AI search tool hype
out there and follow this exact AEO an
SEO playbook to start driving qualified
organic inbound leads, signups, and
demos from SEO as quick as the next 90
days. Over at my agency, Breaking B2B,
we've helped dozens of tech and SAS
companies like RB2B, Proposify, Events,
and many more rank number one on Google
organic and AI search, driving millions
in pipeline and revenue all through AEO
and SEO. So, in this video, I'll share
the exact system so your company can
start dominating AEO and traditional
SEO. Let's dive in. So, step one is all
about defining your money keywords. What
the heck's a money keyword? That
basically means what would a dream
client search for on Google organic or
LLM's AI search chat GBT when they need
your solution right now when they're in
market for your offer? Perhaps they know
the category, they know the solution,
they know the software they need. Maybe
they're comparing vendors or maybe
they're looking to switch away from the
incumbent vendor or perhaps they have a
very crisp and specific problem that
your solution solves. That is a money
keyword. And what you want to do is you
want to map out every way a dream client
could search for your solution. Whether
that's on AI search or Google search, I
recommend crafting something called a
money keyword matrix. So build out your
money keyword matrix. Typically in
simple terms, this has four main
columns. Column one is what's every way
that someone could refer to our service,
software, or solution. Column two is
what are the industries that we serve
super well, have the problem we solve,
they're motivated to solve it, they have
cash to invest in our offer, and we've
sold well to them historically. Column
three is what are our main competitors?
the competitors that always come up on
sales calls, those three, four, five
competitors almost to an annoying level
that we have to compete against or that
folks move away from and over to us. And
column four, what's our dream client's
jobs to be done? Their struggling
moments, the expensive problems they're
trying to solve, or the way they're
getting the job done today, maybe with
Excel spreadsheets or Google Sheets, or
maybe they're cobbling something
together in house until they got so
frustrated it causes churn, it causes
revenue loss, it causes time loss until
they need to find a new better way where
we can position our tools, software, or
service as the painkiller and as the
solution. And I recommend building out
that matrix with, if you're the founder
of a company, you might know this stuff.
if you're a small team running sales
calls and customer success calls, but if
you've got a sales team, customer
success team and marketing team, getting
them involved in the process so they can
really dig into all those solutions, all
those industries, all those competitors
and all those pain points that are going
to be super relevant today for you. But
at a high level, these are going to be
things like best category software or
tools or platforms offer for industry,
competitor alternatives or competitor
pricing or competitor reviews or
competitor A versus competitor B. Now,
you might be thinking, Sam, there's a
ton of other ways that folks could
search for our offer, but we're starting
where there's money. We're starting
right at the bottom of salesunnel.
What's a dream client most likely to
search when they actually need our
offer, our software, our solution? And
in this case, we're going directly when
they are seeking out that solution or
they're looking for the software within
their industry, looking to evaluate
competitors or they're looking to move
away from their current vendor. And
yeah, when you move up funnel, you can
go for integration based search terms,
how-to based search terms, calculator or
free toolbased search terms and that
kind of stuff, which can be good at the
